title: Customer Satisfaction Score (CSAT/NPS) Trend Dashboard
dataSources: `index=business` `sourcetype="nps_survey"` (score, verbatim, channel, product, date), CRM data via DB Connect
spl:
index=business sourcetype="nps_survey" earliest=-90d
| eval category=case(score>=9,"Promoter", score>=7,"Passive", 1=1,"Detractor")
| bin _time span=1w
| stats count as responses,
        sum(eval(if(category="Promoter",1,0))) as promoters,
        sum(eval(if(category="Detractor",1,0))) as detractors,
        avg(score) as avg_score by _time
| eval nps=round(100*(promoters-detractors)/responses, 0)
| table _time, responses, promoters, detractors, nps, avg_score
| sort _time
description: Centralises customer satisfaction metrics вАФ Net Promoter Score, CSAT, CES вАФ alongside operational data, enabling teams to correlate satisfaction dips with specific incidents, releases, or service changes. Executives see customer health at a glance rather than waiting for quarterly survey reports.
value: Centralises customer satisfaction metrics вАФ Net Promoter Score, CSAT, CES вАФ alongside operational data, enabling teams to correlate satisfaction dips with specific incidents, releases, or service changes. Executives see customer health at a glance rather than waiting for quarterly survey reports.
implementation: (1) Ingest survey responses via HEC or DB Connect from your survey platform (Qualtrics, Medallia, SurveyMonkey); (2) include product/service and channel fields for segmentation; (3) schedule weekly NPS calculation; (4) alert when NPS drops below threshold or week-over-week decline exceeds 10 points; (5) add text analytics on verbatim comments using `rex` for common complaint themes.
visualization: Line chart (NPS trend), Single value (current NPS), Pie chart (Promoter/Passive/Detractor split), Word cloud or bar chart (top complaint themes from verbatims).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
вАҐ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686), HEC.
вАҐ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=business` `sourcetype="nps_survey"` (score, verbatim, channel, product, date), CRM data via DB Connect.
вАҐ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 вАФ Configure data collection
(1) Ingest survey responses via HEC or DB Connect from your survey platform (Qualtrics, Medallia, SurveyMonkey); (2) include product/service and channel fields for segmentation; (3) schedule weekly NPS calculation; (4) alert when NPS drops below threshold or week-over-week decline exceeds 10 points; (5) add text analytics on verbatim comments using `rex` for common complaint themes.

Step 2 вАФ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=business sourcetype="nps_survey" earliest=-90d
| eval category=case(score>=9,"Promoter", score>=7,"Passive", 1=1,"Detractor")
| bin _time span=1w
| stats count as responses,
        sum(eval(if(category="Promoter",1,0))) as promoters,
        sum(eval(if(category="Detractor",1,0))) as detractors,
        avg(score) as avg_score by _time
| eval nps=round(100*(promoters-detractors)/responses, 0)
| table _time, responses, promoters, detractors, nps, avg_score
| sort _time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Customer Satisfaction Score (CSAT/NPS) Trend Dashboard** вАФ Centralises customer satisfaction metrics вАФ Net Promoter Score, CSAT, CES вАФ alongside operational data, enabling teams to correlate satisfaction dips with specific incidents, releases, or service changes. Executives see customer health at a glance rather than waiting for quarterly survey reports.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=business` `sourcetype="nps_survey"` (score, verbatim, channel, product, date), CRM data via DB Connect. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686), HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedвАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: business; **sourcetype**: nps_survey. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

вАҐ Scopes the data: index=business, sourcetype="nps_survey", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
вАҐ `eval` defines or adjusts **category** вАФ often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
вАҐ Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
вАҐ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
вАҐ `eval` defines or adjusts **nps** вАФ often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
вАҐ Pipeline stage (see **Customer Satisfaction Score (CSAT/NPS) Trend Dashboard**): table _time, responses, promoters, detractors, nps, avg_score
вАҐ Orders rows with `sort` вАФ combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 вАФ Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 вАФ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (NPS trend), Single value (current NPS), Pie chart (Promoter/Passive/Detractor split), Word cloud or bar chart (top complaint themes from verbatims).
grandmaExplanation: Centralises customer satisfaction metrics вАФ so revenue, usage, and customer trends are visible in near-real-time.
